title: Fully Funded Journalism Fellowship 2026 to Cover the Global Environment Facility Assembly in Samarkand, Uzbekistan
summary: Journalists from low- and middle-income countries have a unique opportunity to report on one of the world’s most significant environmental governance events through a fellowship offered by the Internews Earth Journalism Network (EJN) . The program will support selected reporters to attend and cover the Eighth Global Environment Facility (GEF) Assembly in Samarkand, Uzbekistan. The fellowship is designed to strengthen journalism on environmental finance, climate policy, and global sustainability negotiations by enabling reporters to directly observe and report on high-level discussions shaping the future of global environmental action. Applications for the fellowship are open until March 31, 2026 . Overview of the Fellowship Internews Earth Journalism Network is offering fellowships to five journalists from low- and middle-income countries to attend and report from the Eighth Global Environment Facility Assembly , which will take place in Samarkand , Uzbekistan from May 30 to June 6, 2026 . The fellowship aims to improve global reporting on environmental financing and policy decisions by providing journalists with training, mentorship, and direct access to decision-makers attending the Assembly. Participants will gain firsthand insights into negotiations and policies that influence climate action, biodiversity protection, and sustainable development around the world. Through this initiative, journalists will be able to produce impactful stories that explain how international environmental funding and governance decisions affect their home countries and local communities. About the Global Environment Facility The Global Environment Facility (GEF) is a multilateral environmental fund that supports developing nations in meeting the goals of several international environmental conventions. Since its establishment in 1991 , the GEF has provided more than $27 billion in grants and helped mobilize an additional $155 billion for environmental projects worldwide. These funds support initiatives related to climate mitigation, biodiversity protection, sustainable land use, and pollution control. The GEF works closely with major global agreements such as: Convention on Biological Diversity United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change Stockholm Convention on Persistent Organic Pollutants United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification Minamata Convention on Mercury The GEF family of funds includes several specialized financing mechanisms such as: Least Developed Countries Fund (LDCF) Special Climate Change Fund (SCCF) Global Biodiversity Framework Fund (GBFF) Nagoya Protocol Implementation Fund (NPIF) Capacity-building Initiative for Transparency Trust Fund (CBIT) These funding programs support projects that protect water resources, strengthen agriculture and food security, improve health outcomes, reduce disaster risks, and safeguard fragile ecosystems. About the Eighth GEF Assembly The GEF Assembly is the governing body of the organization and includes representatives from 186 member countries . It meets periodically to review and approve major policy decisions, operational strategies, and amendments affecting global environmental funding. The 2026 Assembly in Samarkand will bring together more than 1,000 delegates , including: Government ministers Environmental leaders and experts Business representatives Civil society organizations Indigenous community leaders Officials from international organizations and GEF agencies Participants will discuss key issues such as: Climate adaptation strategies Private sector engagement in environmental action Financing mechanisms for sustainable development Partnerships to address global ecological challenges The Assembly will feature nine high-level roundtables , multiple side events, field trips, and a Civil Society Forum scheduled for June 3 . Fellowship Activities and Training Selected fellows will receive preparation and mentoring before and during the conference. Prior to the Assembly, EJN will provide resources and reporting tools to help fellows prepare for the event. Once in Samarkand, fellows will participate in specialized activities designed to enhance their coverage of environmental negotiations. These activities include: Orientation sessions on key issues discussed at the Assembly Daily briefings on ongoing discussions Interviews with high-level officials and experts Mentorship from an experienced environmental journalist serving as an EJN trainer Collaboration and networking with other international fellows The trainer will help participants understand complex policy discussions and provide feedback to support high-quality reporting throughout the event. Story Production and Publication Fellows may produce stories in several formats, including: Print journalism Online reporting Radio broadcasts Television or multimedia reports Stories may be published in any language , but fellows must provide an English summary of their work. Additionally, at least one story must be translated into English so it can be shared with a broader international audience. While fellows maintain full editorial independence, published work must acknowledge support from EJN by including the following statement: “This story was produced as part of a reporting fellowship to the Eighth GEF Assembly supported by Internews’ Earth Journalism Network.” Fellows are also free to publish their stories first through their affiliated media outlets. However, EJN and its partners retain the right to edit, republish, broadcast, and distribute the content. Fellowship Benefits The fellowship provides comprehensive financial and logistical support for selected journalists. Covered expenses include: Round-trip economy-class airfare Accommodation in Samarkand Meals during the fellowship period Travel insurance Local transportation Rail transport between Tashkent and Samarkand if necessary Visa fee reimbursement Press accreditation support Logistical assistance throughout the trip Journalists selected for the fellowship are responsible for arranging their own visas and ensuring they meet all travel and attendance requirements. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a professional journalist working for or representing an established media organization in a low- or middle-income country Have prior experience reporting on environmental issues Experience covering climate, biodiversity, environmental negotiations, or environmental finance is an advantage Demonstrate strong English language proficiency Be available to arrive in Samarkand by May 31, 2026 and remain for the entire Assembly Provide a letter of support from an editor or supervisor confirming that the outlet will publish or broadcast the resulting stories Submit recent examples of relevant journalistic work Applicants of all ages are welcome, though preference will be given to journalists aged 35 and under . Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on several factors, including: Proven experience reporting on environmental and climate topics A clear and well-developed reporting plan for the Assembly Potential audience reach of the applicant’s media outlet A formal relationship with an established media organization Diversity in geography and media types among selected fellows Applicants must include a signed letter of support on official media letterhead confirming their outlet’s commitment to publish or broadcast the resulting stories. Application Process Interested journalists must complete their application through the fellowship portal. To apply: Click the Apply Now button on the fellowship page. Log in to your existing account or create a new account if you do not have one. Complete the application form and upload all required supporting documents. Save drafts if necessary and return later to finalize the submission. Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications at least one day before the deadline to avoid technical issues. Late submissions will not be considered. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the fellowship is: March 31, 2026 (11:59 PM Pacific/Niue Time) Successful applicants will be notified by mid-April 2026 . This fellowship provides a valuable opportunity for journalists to gain direct access to global environmental policy discussions while producing impactful reporting that informs audiences about climate finance, environmental governance, and sustainable development initiatives worldwide. Discover more Fellowship opportunities here. The post Fully Funded Journalism Fellowship 2026 to Cover the Global Environment Facility Assembly in Samarkand, Uzbekistan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Stanford Venture Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded Trip to Stanford University)
summary: The Stanford Venture Fellowship 2026 is a fully funded one-week fellowship in the Stanford area, California , bringing together 10 exceptional fellows from around the world to explore entrepreneurship, innovation, and the Silicon Valley ecosystem. The program welcomes students and early professionals aged 14+ who want to gain firsthand exposure to startups, venture capital, research, and the Stanford innovation community. Key Details Host: Stanford Venture Fellowship Location: Stanford Area, California, USA Program Duration: 1 Week Expected Dates: May 2026 Number of Fellows: 10 participants worldwide Funding: Fully Funded (Travel reimbursed) Eligibility: Open to applicants aged 14+ worldwide Participants: High school students, university students, graduate students, and early professionals Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will receive a fully funded experience , including: Travel reimbursement from anywhere in the world Accommodation in the Stanford area Access to structured sessions, workshops, and discussions Networking with Stanford students, founders, and faculty Exposure to Silicon Valley startups and venture capital Connection with a global cohort of fellows Additional logistical details will be shared with selected fellows closer to the program. About the Stanford Venture Fellowship The Stanford Venture Fellowship (SVF) is a one-week in-person program created by Stanford students, alumni, and mentors . The fellowship aims to bring ambitious individuals from around the world into the Stanford and Silicon Valley ecosystem , allowing them to learn directly from founders, researchers, venture capitalists, and innovators. Participants will engage in workshops, mentorship sessions, discussions, and startup visits designed to provide insight into how innovation is built within one of the world’s most influential technology hubs. What Fellows Will Experience During the fellowship week, participants will: Stay in the Stanford area throughout the program Participate in structured workshops and discussions Visit startups and innovation spaces in Silicon Valley Interact directly with Stanford researchers and founders Collaborate with a small cohort of global fellows The program is designed to simulate what it feels like to live and learn inside the Stanford entrepreneurial ecosystem . Example Fellowship Week Sunday – Arrival Arrival and check-in Welcome dinner and introductions Monday – Entering the Ecosystem Opening session on the Stanford ecosystem Campus walk and orientation Workshop on building in ambitious environments Tuesday – Founders Startup Building Founder conversations Startup visits Cohort working sessions Wednesday – Research and Venture Capital Faculty conversation Venture capital insights session Palo Alto exploration Thursday – Mentorship and Skills Technical or strategy workshop Mentorship roundtables Speaker dinner Friday – Program Synthesis Fellow presentations Ecosystem discussions Closing dinner Saturday – Departure Final gathering Departures The final schedule will be shared with selected fellows. Fellowship Origins The Stanford Venture Fellowship grew out of selective initiatives within the Stanford ecosystem. Members of the team have previously led and contributed to programs including the Stanford Cowell Fund , Stanford s student-led quantitative investment fund, which runs a highly selective analyst program, as well as initiatives connected to ASES (Stanford s entrepreneurial society). After several years of running similar initiatives within the United States, the program is now expanding globally to welcome fellows from around the world. Industry Engagement Past events organized by members of the Stanford Venture Fellowship team have included individuals from organizations such as: OpenAI Google Tesla Microsoft Y Combinator Jane Street Goldman Sachs These engagements provide fellows exposure to leaders in technology, finance, and entrepreneurship . Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Must be 14 years or older Open to applicants from any country Students and early professionals are encouraged to apply Eligible participants include: High school students Undergraduate students Graduate students Early-career professionals Applicants under 18 may be able to attend with parent or guardian accompaniment , with travel support provided where appropriate. Why You Should Apply The Stanford Venture Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to: Experience Silicon Valley innovation firsthand Connect with global entrepreneurs and researchers Build entrepreneurship and leadership skills Expand your global professional network Learn inside the Stanford ecosystem With only 10 fellows selected worldwide , the program is highly competitive. How to Apply Applications will open soon. Click here Interested applicants can sign up to receive a notification when applications become available. Still have questions? Visit our FAQ . Partnership inquiries: partners@svfellow.com For more scholarships, fellowships, and global opportunities, visit: https://opportunitiesforyouth.org The post Stanford Venture Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded Trip to Stanford University) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: United Nations Internet Governance Forum (IGF) Fellowship Programme 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland (United Nations)
summary: The Internet Governance Forum (IGF) Fellowship Programme 2026 is now open for applications. This United Nations fellowship opportunity allows professionals from developing countries to work with the IGF Secretariat at the United Nations Office in Geneva, Switzerland and gain hands-on experience in Internet governance, digital policy, and multistakeholder global cooperation . The fellowship is administered by the United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs (UN DESA) and financed through the IGF Trust Fund . Key Details Host Organization: United Nations – Internet Governance Forum (IGF) Secretariat Location: Geneva, Switzerland Programme Duration: 6 months (May – November 2026) Fellowship Type: UN Fellowship Programme Funding: Financed through the IGF Trust Fund (administrative support, insurance, and programme support provided through UN DESA) Application Deadline: 31 March 2026 Applicants must submit their CV, motivation letter, and nomination form to: vacancies@intgovforum.org About the Internet Governance Forum (IGF) The Internet Governance Forum ( IGF ) is a permanent forum of the United Nations initially convened by the United Nations Secretary-General in 2006. Institutionally, it is placed under the Department of Economic and Social Affairs (DESA). The IGF Secretariat supports the implementation of the IGF process, while the development of the annual IGF meeting’s agenda is guided by the IGF Multistakeholder Advisory Group ( MAG ). The Fellowship Programme is part of the IGF ’s mandated objective to develop capacity in Internet governance in developing countries. Fellows are selected through an open-to-all stakeholders application process, implemented in collaboration with the relevant regional IGFs, in accordance with the criteria outlined further below. The UN Fellow will work with the IGF Secretariat, which covers a wide range of activities, such as the clearance process of the renewal of the IGF MAG , implementing the programme for the IGF annual meeting and the intersessional work including capacity development, liaising with the donor’s network to the IGF , closely working with the governmental entities that host the annual IGF meeting, and providing substantive Internet governance-related information to the UN DESA and Executive Office of the United Nations Secretary-General. The IGF Fellowship Programme is financed through the IGF Trust Fund and processed by UN DESA. The objective of the programme is to contribute to enhancing the effectiveness of development aid by fostering the participation of skilled professionals from the developing countries, least developed countries (LDCs), landlocked developing countries (LLDCs) and transitional economies in analytical, research and operational activities of Internet governance. Through this initiative, Fellows are integrated in the work of the IGF Secretariat based at the United Nations Office at Geneva, Switzerland. They are expected to enhance their professional competence and knowledge, enrich their work methods and techniques and, after their fellowship, assist their country community in their local Internet governance requirements. There are up to two (2) programme cycles within the year which last six (6) months each. It may start within the specified six (6) months of the year or from January to June and/or from July to December of the year. The selection of candidates, as per the requirements outlined further below, is done in consultations with the relevant national and regional IGF coordinators and guidelines provided by the IGF Secretariat’s Head of Office. The selection of regions eligible to the IGF Fellowship programme is done by rotation of regions. A relevant regional and sub-regional IGF initiatives will be consulted throughout the process to facilitate outreach. Interested candidates from all stakeholder and regional groups are invited to apply for a fellowship by sending their CV, a motivation letter and this nomination form to vacancies [at] intgovforum [dot] org by 31 March 2026. Duties, responsibilities and output expectations Under the guidance and supervision of the IGF Secretariat’s Head of Office, the fellow will perform the following functions: Provide substantive and organizational support to the implementation of project objectives, as assigned by the Head of IGF Secretariat, including liaison and dialogue with IGF networks membership and other stakeholders, drafting programme papers and reports; due date, continuous. Do research work and focus specifically on development issues related to Internet governance, in particular, those affecting developing countries, least developed countries (LDCs), landlocked developing countries (LLDCs) and transitional economies; due date, continuous. In preparation for the IGF 2026, contribute to the substantive elements of the agenda of the IGF Multistakeholder Advisory Group ( MAG ); due date, continuous. Provide support to the preparatory process for organizing the 21st annual IGF meeting, to be hosted in the last quarter of 2026; due date, continuous. Perform other substantive tasks as assigned by the supervisor. The above will imply interaction with the staff working within the unit/section, staff of international organizations working on related issues, academics and representatives of civil society groups, NGOs, technical community and the business sector. In performing these duties, fellows must comply with the requirements (guidelines) outlined in the Letter of Acceptance and dedicate their full time to the fellowship programme. They must not engage in political, commercial or any other activities other than those governed by the programme. They must not accept another fellowship programme, work, and non- IGF Secretariat meeting participation while under the IGF Fellowship programme. The fellows have no vacation leave except sick leave supported by a medical certificate. Duration The proposed duration of the fellowship programme is six (6) months between May and November 2026. Learning elements Upon the completion of the assignment, it is expected that the UN Fellow will be able to deliver the following outputs: Apply rules and procedures of multilateral cooperation in general and multistakeholder processes, in particular; Better understand Internet governance issues and processes; Draft position papers, studies and reports; Better understand the role of all stakeholders (Government, civil society, private sector, technical community) in Internet governance arrangements; Exchange information and capabilities with participants in the IGF . Content and methodology of the supervision The UN Fellow s work will be continuously supervised through monthly regular programme meetings. The UN Fellow will be required to submit a monthly progress report to the assigned supervisor to assess Fellow’s performance. Weekly or ad-hoc interactions with the assigned supervisor will be considered depending on the work priority. Travels The Fellow will not be required to undertake missions to neighbouring countries or to other duty stations. Duty station is in Geneva. Qualifications and experience Education: University degree in ICT or international affairs related with a strong emphasis on technology and/or telecommunication policy is required. Work experience: A minimum of 2 years of relevant professional work experience in Internet governance related processes is required. Experience in policy making will be considered an advantage. Languages: Fluency in English (both oral and written) is required. Working knowledge of French is desirable. Other Necessary Skills: Ability to write clearly and concisely on Internet governance issues; good understanding of the development aspects of Internet governance issues; skills in developing sources for data collection and data analysis; ability to plan and organize his/her own work in support of and in coordination with other members of the team; sound knowledge of Internet and basic software for website management, word processing and spreadsheets; good interpersonal and communication skills; ability to establish and maintain effective working relations with people of different national and cultural backgrounds. Competencies Professionalism: Knowledge of Internet governance issues. Ability to conduct research and provide recommendations on Internet governance issues. Ability to demonstrate conceptual, analytical and evaluative skills and conduct independent research and analysis, identifying and assessing issues, formulating options and making conclusions and recommendations. Shows pride in work and in achievements; demonstrates professional competence and mastery of subject matter; is conscientious and efficient in meeting commitments, observing deadlines and achieving results; is motivated by professional rather than personal concerns. Teamwork: Works collaboratively with colleagues to achieve organizational goals; solicits input by genuinely valuing others’ ideas and expertise; is willing to learn from others; places team agenda before personal agenda; supports and acts in accordance with final group decision, even when such decisions may not entirely reflect own position; shares credit for team accomplishments and accepts joint responsibility for team shortcomings. Communication: Speaks and writes clearly and effectively; listens to others, correctly interprets messages from others and responds appropriately; asks questions to clarify, and exhibits interest in having two-way communication; tailor language, tone, style and format to match audience; demonstrates openness in sharing information and keeping people informed. Planning and Organizing: Develops clear goals that are consistent with agreed strategies; identifies priority activities and assignments; adjusts priorities as required; allocates appropriate amount of time and resources for completing work; foresees risks and allows for contingencies when planning; monitors and adjusts plans and actions as necessary; uses time efficiently. Client Orientation: Considers all those to whom services are provided to be “clients” and seeks to see things from clients’ point of view; establishes and maintains productive partnerships with clients by gaining their trust and respect; identifies clients’ needs and matches them to appropriate solutions. Commitment to Continuous Learning: Keeps abreast of new developments in own occupation/profession; actively seeks to develop oneself professionally and personally. Respect for Diversity: Ability to work effectively with multi-disciplinary and multi-cultural tams. Management arrangements The Fellow will perform his/her duties under the general guidance of the IGF Secretariat’s Head of Office in Geneva. UN DESA Training and Fellowships Section will be the approving office of proposed candidates to the IGF Fellowship Programme. It will be responsible for all the administrative issues pertaining to fellowships, including the security aspects and the medical insurance of the fellows. These offices will be in close contact with the UNDP country offices. Recipient organization is responsible to provide fellows with the necessary facilities to properly perform their assignments, including office space, access to a computer and e-mail address. Reporting Fellows’ performance will be assessed through the analysis of their monthly and final reports. At the end of the fellowship, the fellow is required to submit a progress report indicating the work accomplished during the assignment. The report will help evaluate the overall fellowship period. The Fellow’s direct supervisor will also submit written evaluation of fellow’s performance to UN DESA Capacity Development Office. Fellows are not staff members. As such, they do not enjoy the benefits and entitlements stipulated in the UN Staff Rules and Regulations. Fellows are governed by the UN DESA Policies and Procedures on Fellowships, Study Tours and Group Training which can be found on-line at: http://esa.un.org/techcoop/fellowships/index.html Information about living conditions in Switzerland Switzerland, officially the Swiss Confederation, is a country situated in western, central, and southern Europe. It consists of 26 cantons, and the city of Bern as the seat of the federal authorities. The sovereign state is a federal republic bordered by Italy to the south, France to the west, Germany to the north, and Austria and Liechtenstein to the east. Switzerland is a landlocked country geographically divided between the Alps, the Swiss Plateau and the Jura, spanning a total area of 41,285 km2 (15,940 sq mi) (land area 39,997 km2 (15,443 sq mi)). The Swiss population of approximately 8.5 million people is concentrated mostly on the plateau. Switzerland s public transport network is safe and efficient. Trains, trams, buses and boats cover the entire country. There are also extensive cycling routes. The rail network in Switzerland is well organized and the Swiss Federal Railways, offers a multitude of options. The bus and tram system is also well organized and the http://www.tpg.ch/ offers detailed information. A valid passport with visa is required to enter the country. It is recommended to consult the local embassy for more information: https://www.eda.admin.ch/en . The climate in Switzerland is moderate, with clearly distinguishable seasons. For more information about the weather, consult: https://www.meteoswiss.admin.ch/home/climate/the-climate-of-switzerland.html The official currency of the country is: Swiss franc. Credit cards are generally accepted everywhere. Switzerland has four national languages: German, French, Italian and Romansh. English, though not an official language, is often used to bridge the divides, and a significant proportion of official documentation is available in English. Generally, Switzerland is considered to be a safe country. Source: https://www.intgovforum.org/en/content/igf-2026-fellowship-programme The post United Nations Internet Governance Forum (IGF) Fellowship Programme 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland (United Nations) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Italian Government Scholarships 2026–2027 for Foreign Students and Italians Living Abroad (IRE): Fully Funded Study in Italy Opportunity
summary: The Italian Government Scholarships 2026–2027 are now open for applications. Offered by the Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation (MAECI) , these scholarships support foreign students and Italian citizens living abroad (IRE) who want to pursue study, research, training, or Italian language and culture programmes in Italy . This is a major opportunity for international students seeking to study in Italy with funding , gain access to top Italian universities and institutions, and experience Italy’s rich academic and cultural environment. The application deadline is March 26, 2026 . What Are the Italian Government Scholarships 2026–2027? The MAECI scholarships for the 2026–2027 academic year are designed to promote: international cultural, scientific, and technological cooperation the Italian language and culture abroad Italy’s higher education system academic mobility and global engagement These scholarships are available only for on-site study in Italy at public or legally recognized private Italian institutions . Who Can Apply? The scholarship is open to: Foreign students from eligible countries Italian citizens living abroad (IRE) in selected countries Selection is based on citizenship , and applicants may apply through one country only . Scholarships reserved for Italian citizens living abroad (IRE) are available only to applicants who hold Italian citizenship only and live in one of the following countries: Argentina, Australia, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Egypt, Ethiopia, France, Germany, Kenya, Lebanon, Morocco, Peru, United Kingdom, Senegal, Spain, South Africa, Tunisia, Uruguay, and Venezuela. Applicants with dual citizenship must apply through the selection process of their foreign beneficiary country and indicate that foreign citizenship on the application portal. Applicants with multiple foreign citizenships may apply through only one beneficiary country of which they are citizens. Types of Italian Government Scholarships Available 1. Master’s Degree Scholarships Scholarships are available for Master’s Degree programmes (Laurea Magistrale, 2nd cycle) in Italy. Duration: 9 months Renewable: Yes, potentially Requirement: Applicants must hold a qualification equivalent to an Italian Bachelor’s degree Some programmes may require admission tests or pre-enrolment . 2. AFAM Scholarships These scholarships support studies in Higher Education for Fine Arts, Music and Dance (AFAM) at both 1st and 2nd cycle levels. Duration: 9 months Renewable: Yes, potentially Applicants must meet the academic requirements of the chosen institution. 3. PhD Scholarships in Italy Students applying for PhD programmes in Italy can also apply. Duration: 9 months Renewable: Yes, potentially Applicants must meet institutional requirements and must have passed the admission test . 4. Research Project Scholarships These scholarships support research projects under academic supervision , including co-tutorships. Duration: 9 months Renewable: No Research can be carried out at: Italian universities public research institutions recognized museums archives Applicants must submit: a research project an invitation letter from the supervisor 5. Italofonia Scholarships for Italian Language and Culture Courses These scholarships are for intensive Italian language and culture courses . Duration: 3 months Minimum Italian language level required: A2 CEFR 6. “Costruiamo il futuro” Scholarship Renewals This category is reserved only for students renewing the “Costruiamo il futuro” scholarship awarded in the previous cycle. Duration: 9 months Renewable: Yes, potentially Eligibility Criteria for Italian Government Scholarships 2026–2027 Applicants must upload a valid passport or identity document when registering on the portal. Academic Requirements Applicants must hold the qualification required for admission to the programme they select. Master’s and AFAM applicants must have the required academic qualification PhD applicants must upload their admission letter to the PhD programme Research applicants must submit the supervisor’s invitation letter and research proposal Language course applicants must meet the Italian language requirement at the time of application Age Limits Applicants must meet the following age requirements by March 26, 2026 : Master’s, AFAM, Italian language courses, and 1.6 renewals: must not be over 28 years old PhD applicants: must not be over 30 years old Research project applicants: must not be over 40 years old Language Requirements For Italian-Taught Programmes Applicants for Italian-taught Master’s and AFAM programmes must demonstrate Italian proficiency at B2 level or above under the CEFR . For English-Taught Programmes Applicants for English-taught Master’s and AFAM programmes must demonstrate English proficiency at B2 level or above . Accepted proof may include: a language certificate proof that the previous degree was entirely taught in English for applicants from English-speaking countries, a self-declaration of B2 English proficiency For Italian Language and Culture Courses Applicants must show at least A2 level Italian proficiency . Exemptions Applicants for PhD programmes and research projects do not need to prove language proficiency for the scholarship application, though institutions may still have their own requirements. Who Is Not Eligible? Applicants are not eligible if they: are enrolled in or obtained a secondary school diploma in Italy are enrolled in or obtained a degree from an Italian higher education institution in Italy obtained a degree from a foreign institution located in Italy carried out research projects with a MAECI scholarship in the last 5 academic years seek to enrol in a year other than the first year of the chosen programme Other exclusions include: employees of the Italian Public Administration and their first-degree descendants students enrolled part-time students enrolled beyond the legal duration of their degree programme The scholarships are generally not available for: most Bachelor’s degree programmes single-cycle degree programmes first- and second-level university Master’s programmes specialization courses one-year university courses single courses Scholarship Benefits For 9-Month Scholarships Winners under categories 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, and 1.6 receive: €10,800 total This amount is paid in three instalments to an Italian bank account in the recipient’s name: €5,500 from November 2026 €2,300 from February 2027 €3,000 from June 2027 For 3-Month Italian Language Scholarships Winners under category 1.5 receive: €3,600 total This is paid in one instalment . Scholarship Duration 9-Month Scholarships For Master’s, AFAM, PhD, research, and renewal categories: Start date: November 1, 2026 End date: July 31, 2027 3-Month Language Scholarships These must: start from January 1, 2027 end by September 30, 2027 begin on the first day and end on the last day of the relevant quarter Tuition Fees and Health Insurance Some Italian universities may exempt scholarship recipients from tuition and enrolment fees , but this depends on the institution. Students should verify this directly with their chosen university. Important notes: Regional fees must still be paid by the student Italian language and culture courses are not exempt from tuition fees Scholarship recipients are also covered by a collective health insurance policy arranged by MAECI for illness or accidents during the scholarship period. Renewal of MAECI Scholarships Students who received a MAECI scholarship in 2025–2026 may apply for renewal for 2026–2027 if they are continuing the same full-time programme at the same institution . Priority is given to students who are duly enrolled and can show at least 15 university credits (CFU) earned at the time of application. Renewals are not available for: research projects Italian language and culture courses Special “Attanasio” Scholarships For the 2026–2027 academic year, MAECI is also reserving three 9-month scholarships in memory of Ambassador Luca Attanasio , Vittorio Iacovacci , and Mustapha Milambo . These are for applicants from: Morocco Nigeria Democratic Republic of Congo They are intended for students pursuing studies in fields such as: international relations development cooperation international humanitarian law human rights law WikiAfrica Higher Education Initiative African MAECI scholarship recipients who speak African languages may also have the opportunity to participate in the WikiAfrica Higher Education Initiative , developed with Aurora Foundation , Moleskine Foundation , and Wikimedia Foundation . This initiative supports the creation and sharing of specialized knowledge on Africa online. How to Apply for the Italian Government Scholarship 2026–2027 Applications must be submitted online only through the Study in Italy portal https://studyinitaly.esteri.it Applicants should use an email address they check regularly, since further communication will be sent there. Application Deadline The final deadline is: March 26, 2026 at 14:00 Italian time Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early and not wait until the final day. Supplementary Documents Some applicants may later be asked to upload supplementary documents, including: academic qualification documents PhD admission letters language certificates This upload option will only be enabled for applicants who indicated the need for it and who appear in the merit ranking. Supplementary Document Deadline If requested, supplementary documents must be uploaded by: July 1, 2026 at 14:00 Italian time Selection Process The scholarship selection process includes: comparative evaluation of applications interview for shortlisted candidates publication of the merit ranking upload of supplementary documents if requested acceptance or decline of the scholarship issuance and acceptance of the awarding letter Important Notes for Selected Candidates Scholarship recipients must: complete pre-enrolment and enrolment obtain a tax identification number (codice fiscale) if required apply for a study visa where necessary ensure all documentation is completed before the scholarship start date Scholarship Incompatibility This scholarship is not compatible with other scholarships offered by the Italian Government or other Italian public institutions . However, it is compatible with: Erasmus Erasmus+ internships abroad programmes funded by international institutions Still, the scholarship will not be paid during periods spent studying, researching, or interning outside Italy . Final Thoughts The Italian Government Scholarships 2026–2027 offer an excellent chance for international students and Italians living abroad to pursue graduate study, PhD research, AFAM programmes, or Italian language training in Italy with financial support. With funding of up to €10,800 , health insurance coverage, and access to Italian higher education institutions, this is one of the top study in Italy scholarships for international students in 2026 . Anyone interested should review the requirements carefully, confirm eligibility, prepare documents early, and submit the application before March 26, 2026 . Application Link Apply through the Study in Italy portal . Useful References Study in Italy portal https://studyinitaly.esteri.it Universitaly universitaly.it Europass CEFR framework https://europa.eu/europass/en/common-european-framework-reference Il visto per l Italia https://www.mur.gov.it/it/aree-tematiche/universita/le-universita/universita-non-stataliriconosciute https://www.mur.gov.it/it/aree-tematiche/afam/gli-istituti https://ardi.cimea.it/it Website guidelines: https://studyinitaly.esteri.it/Static/GuidaAlSito FAQ: https://studyinitaly.esteri.it/Static/FAQ The Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation has published a call for applications for scholarships offered by the Italian Government to foreign students who are interested in pursuing graduate courses in Italy in the academic year 2026/2027. For Uganda and Rwanda, three annual scholarships will be available; for Burundi, one annual scholarship. The call for applications is available in Italian by clicking here , and in English by clicking here . All applications must be submitted exclusively through the portal studyinitaly.esteri.it and must be received by the 26th of march 2026. The post Italian Government Scholarships 2026–2027 for Foreign Students and Italians Living Abroad (IRE): Fully Funded Study in Italy Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: BeChangeMaker 2026: Fully Funded Social Entrepreneurship Programme for Young Innovators
summary: Applications are now open for BeChangeMaker 2026 , a global social entrepreneurship programme celebrating 10 years of empowering young innovators to turn bold ideas into impactful ventures. Launched by WorldSkills and the HP Foundation , with support from UNESCO-UNEVOC , the BeChangeMaker programme has helped hundreds of young entrepreneurs worldwide develop solutions addressing major social and environmental challenges aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) . If you are a young changemaker with an idea that can improve your community or the world, BeChangeMaker 2026 offers a powerful platform to accelerate your social enterprise and connect with global mentors, investors, and innovators. Key Details Programme: BeChangeMaker 2026 Organizers: WorldSkills HP Foundation Supported by: UNESCO-UNEVOC Programme Duration: April – September 2026 Eligibility Age: 18 – 35 years Team Size: Up to 5 members (or apply individually) Final Pitch Location: WorldSkills Shanghai 2026, China Application Deadline: 27 March 2026 What is BeChangeMaker? BeChangeMaker is a global online training programme designed for young entrepreneurs and innovators aged 18–35 who want to transform their ideas into sustainable businesses that create positive social impact. Since its launch in 2017 , the programme has supported youth from around the world by providing: Entrepreneurial training Mentorship and coaching Global networking opportunities Access to innovation resources Opportunities to pitch ideas to international audiences The 2026 edition marks the programme’s 10th anniversary , highlighting a decade of youth-led innovation and entrepreneurship. What Participants Receive Selected teams will benefit from a comprehensive programme designed to help turn ideas into real ventures. 1. Online Learning through HP LIFE Participants gain access to HP LIFE , a world-class online learning platform offering business and entrepreneurship training tailored for innovators at all stages. 2. Expert Coaching and Mentorship Teams receive personalized coaching and mentoring from experts in entrepreneurship, innovation, and business development. 3. Global Networking Opportunities Participants connect with a global community of entrepreneurs, investors, and supporters , opening doors to partnerships and collaboration. 4. Pitch Opportunities and Cash Prizes The programme culminates in a live pitch competition where the top teams present their ideas to a global audience and compete for prizes. Final Pitch at WorldSkills Shanghai 2026 The top five teams will be selected through a series of online pitching rounds and invited to present their ideas at the WorldSkills Conference 2026 in Shanghai, China . During the final stage: Teams will pitch to an international panel of experts and investors The best social innovation ideas will compete for cash prizes Participants will gain global exposure and recognition The programme will culminate in a live pitch final at WorldSkills Shanghai 2026, during WorldSkills Conference 2026 , where the top five teams – selected through a series of online pitches – will present their ideas to an international audience and compete for cash prizes. Programme Timeline Here are the key dates for BeChangeMaker 2026 : Application Deadline: 27 March 2026 Top 30 Teams Announced: 1 April 2026 Programme Start Date: 7 April 2026 Bootcamps Final Pitch: 22–27 September 2026 Location: WorldSkills Shanghai 2026 Eligibility Criteria To apply for BeChangeMaker 2026 , applicants must: Be between 18 and 35 years old Have an innovative idea addressing social or environmental challenges Be passionate about entrepreneurship and sustainable development Apply as a team of up to five members or individually Have the motivation to develop a social enterprise aligned with the SDGs How to Apply Follow these steps to submit your application: Create a Team Form a team of up to five members and choose a team leader, or apply individually. Record a One-Minute Video Introduce your team and explain your project idea. Submit the Online Application Complete the application form on the BeChangeMaker 2026 official website and upload your video. Fill in the form on the BeChangeMaker 2026 website and submit your application . APPLY 📅 Deadline: 27 March 2026 Why You Should Apply Participating in BeChangeMaker 2026 offers an incredible opportunity to: Develop your social entrepreneurship skills Turn innovative ideas into impactful businesses Learn from global mentors and experts Connect with young innovators worldwide Gain international exposure and recognition Pitch your idea at a global event in Shanghai Many past participants have gone on to launch successful social enterprises, attract investment, and build lasting global networks. Applications are open until 27 March 2026. Learn more about BeChangeMaker and find inspiration from previous participants . The BeChangeMaker programme has become one of the most influential global initiatives supporting youth social entrepreneurs . With its tenth edition in 2026, the programme continues to empower the next generation of innovators working to solve some of the world’s most pressing challenges. If you have a bold idea that can create social or environmental impact , this is your chance to take it to the next level. Apply now and join a global community of changemakers shaping the future. title: Fully Funded UNICEF Internship Programme – No Fees, Paid, Open to Students & Graduates Worldwide
summary: Begin your professional journey with one of the world’s most trusted organizations—UNICEF. The UNICEF Internship Programme offers fully funded internship opportunities for students and recent graduates across the globe. This prestigious program provides hands-on work experience, exposure to multicultural environments, and the opportunity to make a real impact in the lives of children worldwide. About the UNICEF Internship Programme The UNICEF Internship Programme supports emerging professionals by offering fully funded, structured, and impactful internships that promote personal and career development in the humanitarian and development sectors. 🔹 Host Organization: United Nations International Children’s Emergency Fund (UNICEF) 🔹 Internship Locations: Multiple countries worldwide (availability depends on current postings) Who Can Apply? This global program is open to candidates who meet the following criteria: Must be at least 18 years old Currently enrolled in an undergraduate, graduate, or PhD program , or graduated within the past two years Proficient in English, French, or Spanish Strong academic record and motivation Familiarity with the working language of the country office where you re applying No immediate relatives (e.g., parent, sibling) employed by UNICEF or within your reporting line Internship Duration and Structure Length : 6 to 26 weeks Type : Full-time or part-time (depending on the role and office) Setting : Some roles may be remote, while others require in-office presence What’s Included – Financial and Professional Support UNICEF internships are fully funded and offer a comprehensive support package: Monthly stipend to cover living expenses Lump sum for travel and visa costs , when applicable Grant funding may be available for specific internship-related projects Mentorship and supervision from UNICEF professionals Networking and multicultural experience through collaboration with global teams How to Apply for a UNICEF Internship Step-by-step application guide: Visit the UNICEF Careers page and navigate to the Internship Opportunities section. You can find current opportunities on our vacancies page . Browse available internships based on your academic and professional interests. Create and complete an online profile via the UNICEF recruitment system. Build a compelling profile and get your dream job with these application and assessment tips . Submit your CV and a tailored cover letter . Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted for interviews. Important: Internship deadlines vary by posting . Apply early and monitor the website regularly for updates. Customize your application to match the job description and responsibilities. Include relevant coursework, experiences, or research that align with UNICEF’s mission. What Past Interns Are Saying “UNICEF offered me the opportunity to grow both professionally and personally, and work on global issues that matter.” — Rudolf Larnyoh, HR Intern, New York “Despite working remotely, I felt fully integrated and supported by my team.” — Eve Woogen, UNICEF Pacific Intern, Suva, Fiji Frequently Asked Questions What kind of tasks will I be assigned? Interns contribute to real projects, including research, data analysis, advocacy, communications, and program support. Tasks vary based on your background and the needs of the office. Is the internship remote or in-person? It depends. While many internships are in-person, some allow for remote or hybrid arrangements. Do interns get hired full-time? While there’s no guarantee, UNICEF internships are highly regarded and may open doors to full-time roles within UNICEF or other UN agencies. How can institutions partner with the internship program? Academic institutions or organizations interested in collaboration can reach out via internship@unicef.org . Have more questions? Email: internship@unicef.org Begin Your UNICEF Internship Journey Today The UNICEF Internship Programme is more than a career opportunity—it s your gateway to meaningful impact, global exposure, and lifelong connections. Whether you’re a student or a recent graduate, this is your chance to learn from top professionals, serve communities in need, and become a part of UNICEF’s global mission. 🔗 Explore Current Internship Openings at UNICEF The post Fully Funded UNICEF Internship Programme – No Fees, Paid, Open to Students Graduates Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Senior Research Manager Position at Harvard University: Join the Center for Education Policy Research
summary: Harvard University is seeking an experienced Senior Research Manager to join the Center for Education Policy Research (CEPR) at the Harvard Graduate School of Education (HGSE) . This opportunity is ideal for professionals with a strong background in quantitative research, education policy, and data analysis who are passionate about improving education systems through evidence-based decision-making. The position offers a competitive salary ranging from $100,000 to $105,000 per year , a hybrid work structure, and the opportunity to collaborate with education leaders, researchers, and policymakers around the world. The role focuses on managing high-impact research projects that support education policy development and school improvement initiatives. About Harvard University and the Harvard Graduate School of Education Working at Harvard University means joining a global academic community committed to advancing knowledge, fostering innovation, and addressing some of the world’s most pressing challenges. Harvard promotes a collaborative and inclusive workplace where employees can develop their skills while contributing to meaningful research and societal progress. The Harvard Graduate School of Education (HGSE) is widely recognized for its dedication to transforming education through research, teaching, and policy leadership. HGSE brings together educators, scholars, and professionals who share a common goal: improving educational opportunities and outcomes for learners worldwide. Many professionals choose HGSE because of its strong mission-driven environment, outstanding leadership, and vibrant community that values diversity, collaboration, and intellectual growth. About the Center for Education Policy Research (CEPR) The Center for Education Policy Research (CEPR) plays a crucial role in advancing education reform through data-driven research. The center collaborates with school districts, charter school networks, state education agencies, and international partners to develop rigorous research that informs education policy and strategic decision-making. CEPR’s core philosophy is that education leaders should rely on evidence rather than assumptions when making decisions that affect students, teachers, and school systems. By applying advanced research methods and data analytics, the center helps education institutions understand what works, what does not, and how systems can be improved. Through innovative research models and partnerships, CEPR aims to transform education systems and ensure that all students have the opportunity to learn and thrive. Job Summary: Senior Research Manager The Senior Research Manager (SRM) will play a central role in managing and advancing CEPR’s research initiatives. The position involves overseeing analytical work across multiple projects, guiding research teams, and ensuring the quality and accuracy of all research outputs. One of the key responsibilities of the role will involve managing projects related to a research partnership with an education nonprofit organization in the Philippines and its government partners . These projects aim to support school improvement efforts and develop a long-term research agenda that strengthens education systems. In addition to managing international projects, the Senior Research Manager may contribute to other CEPR initiatives and supervise Research Analysts across multiple studies. Key Responsibilities The Senior Research Manager will oversee the full research lifecycle, from project design to publication. Major responsibilities include the following: Leading Research and Analytics The SRM will lead the analytical work across several research projects, including: Developing research questions and study designs Writing grant proposals and securing funding Managing data collection and analysis Producing reports and academic publications The role requires strong expertise in quantitative methods and statistical analysis to ensure research findings are accurate and impactful. Managing Research Teams The Senior Research Manager will supervise Research Analysts and support their professional development. This includes mentoring analysts, providing training in research methods, and organizing workshops to strengthen analytical and technical skills. Team leadership is a central aspect of the role, ensuring that research staff develop their expertise while contributing effectively to ongoing projects. Training and Technical Support Another major component of the role involves providing technical guidance on research methods and statistical techniques. The SRM will train both internal team members and external education agency staff on selecting appropriate analytical approaches and using data tools effectively. Project Planning and Coordination The Senior Research Manager will be responsible for ensuring that research projects are completed successfully and on schedule. Key tasks include: Creating project work plans Coordinating team activities across multiple research initiatives Managing project deadlines Leading collaborative meetings between researchers, project directors, and principal investigators The role also requires working closely with leadership teams and external partners to develop strategies for future research initiatives. Ensuring Research Quality and Integrity Maintaining high research standards is essential for CEPR’s mission. The Senior Research Manager will design rigorous studies, identify potential weaknesses in research designs, and ensure that all analyses meet strict quality standards. Responsibilities also include reviewing statistical code, verifying analytical outputs, and establishing protocols that ensure research findings are reproducible and transparent. Conducting Advanced Data Analysis The SRM will also contribute directly to analytical work when necessary, including complex statistical programming and data analysis. This involves managing large-scale education datasets and using advanced statistical tools to answer critical research questions. Publishing and Presenting Research The Senior Research Manager will contribute to research publications and present findings at conferences, meetings, and professional forums. Communicating research insights to both technical and non-technical audiences is a key responsibility of the position. Required Qualifications Applicants must meet several core qualifications to be considered for the role. Educational Background Candidates must hold an advanced degree (master’s level or higher) in one of the following fields: Education Public Policy Economics Statistics Alternatively, candidates with at least five years of directly related professional experience may also qualify. Professional Experience Applicants must demonstrate: At least two years of management experience leading a team A minimum of five years of experience in quantitative research , including data management, analysis, and report writing Strong experience with Stata statistical software Experience working with large-scale education datasets Candidates should also demonstrate the ability to manage multiple complex projects simultaneously and effectively communicate research findings to diverse audiences. Preferred Qualifications Although not mandatory, several additional qualifications are highly desirable for this role. These include: A PhD in quantitative social science methods Experience training others in data analysis and programming Experience collaborating with state education agencies, K–12 school districts, or higher education institutions Knowledge of program evaluation methods , including causal inference and impact evaluation Experience managing research partnerships and engaging stakeholders Experience completing full research projects from concept to publication Additional programming expertise in R or Python Candidates who can translate technical findings into accessible materials for non-technical audiences will have a significant advantage. APPLY HERE Recruitment Process Candidates who pass the initial screening stage will be required to complete several evaluation steps, including: A case study assessment Submission of sample Stata code A writing sample Academic transcripts Final-stage candidates will also present their previous research work. Applicants must submit a cover letter and resume combined into a single PDF document , clearly stating that they meet the required qualifications. Work Structure and Location The Senior Research Manager position follows a hybrid work model . Employees will work: Three days per week in the Cambridge office Two days remotely The work schedule and location may be adjusted based on operational needs and university policies. It is important to note that Harvard University cannot provide visa sponsorship for this position . Salary and Contract Details The position offers a competitive compensation package. Key details include: Salary: $100,000 – $105,000 annually, depending on experience Work schedule: 35 hours per week Contract duration: One-year term appointment with strong possibility of renewal Salary grade: Level 057 Benefits and Employee Support Harvard provides a comprehensive benefits package designed to support employees’ wellbeing and professional development. Key benefits include: Generous paid leave and parental leave Medical, dental, and vision insurance starting on the first day of employment Retirement plans with university contributions Mental health and wellbeing resources Support programs for families and caregivers Tuition assistance and professional development opportunities Commuter benefits and campus discounts These benefits help employees maintain a healthy balance between their professional responsibilities and personal lives. Equal Opportunity Commitment Harvard University is committed to maintaining a workplace that is inclusive, equitable, and free from discrimination. The university actively seeks talent from diverse backgrounds and ensures that all employees are treated fairly regardless of race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, religion, disability, veteran status, or other protected characteristics. This commitment to diversity strengthens Harvard’s academic mission and supports a thriving academic community. Conclusion The Senior Research Manager position at Harvard University’s Center for Education Policy Research presents a unique opportunity for experienced researchers to contribute to meaningful education reforms. By combining rigorous research methods with real-world partnerships, the role allows professionals to influence education policies and practices at both national and international levels. With competitive compensation, a supportive work environment, and the opportunity to collaborate with leading scholars and policymakers, this role is well suited for individuals committed to improving education through research and data-driven decision-making. Discover more similar Opportunities here. The post Senior Research Manager Position at Harvard University: Join the Center for Education Policy Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Princess Sabeeka Global Award for Women’s Empowerment 2026: A $100,000 Global Opportunity Recognizing Leaders Advancing Gender Equality
summary: Across the world, gender equality and women’s empowerment remain central pillars for sustainable development, social progress, and inclusive economic growth. To recognize outstanding efforts that advance these goals, the Princess Sabeeka Bint Ibrahim Al Khalifa Global Award for Women’s Empowerment was established as one of the most prestigious international recognitions for initiatives that transform the lives of women and girls. The award celebrates innovative programs, impactful policies, and visionary leadership that contribute to empowering women across different sectors of society. Through this global initiative, governments, organizations, and individuals working to promote gender equality receive international recognition, financial support, and a platform to showcase their impact. With four awards worth $100,000 each, the program highlights successful models that demonstrate how women’s empowerment contributes to sustainable development and stronger societies worldwide. About the Princess Sabeeka Global Award for Women’s Empowerment The Princess Sabeeka Global Award for Women’s Empowerment was launched by the Kingdom of Bahrain in partnership with UN Women to celebrate initiatives that empower women and girls around the world. The award recognizes innovative approaches and successful projects that promote gender equality in public policy, social development, and economic participation. The award is designed to: Highlight the contributions of individuals and organizations advancing women’s empowerment. Encourage creative and innovative solutions that support gender equality. Recognize successful programs that promote women’s participation in society. Inspire global collaboration toward inclusive development and equality. By showcasing powerful initiatives and leaders who are making a difference, the award aims to strengthen international momentum toward achieving gender equality and sustainable development. Prize Value and Benefits One of the most attractive aspects of this opportunity is the financial and reputational reward that comes with winning the award. Each selected winner receives: US$100,000 prize money Global recognition for their work Increased international visibility Opportunities to showcase their initiative on global platforms Enhanced credibility for scaling and expanding their programs There are four winners, each representing a different category of contribution toward women’s empowerment. Award Categories The Princess Sabeeka Global Award recognizes achievements across four main sectors: 1. Public Sector This category recognizes government institutions and public agencies that have implemented policies or programs that significantly advance women’s empowerment and gender equality. 2. Private Sector Private companies and business organizations that actively promote gender equality within their workforce, leadership structures, and corporate strategies can apply under this category. 3. Civil Society Non-governmental organizations, community groups, foundations, and civil society networks that advocate for women’s rights and empowerment are eligible here. 4. Individual Champions This category celebrates outstanding individuals who have dedicated their careers or initiatives to promoting women’s empowerment and gender equality. Each category recognizes innovative and impactful initiatives that demonstrate measurable results in advancing women s rights and opportunities. Who Can Apply The award is open to applicants worldwide and encourages participation from individuals and institutions that have implemented meaningful initiatives aimed at empowering women. Eligible applicants include: Individual advocates for women’s empowerment Civil society organizations and NGOs Government institutions and public sector bodies Private sector companies and corporate initiatives Applicants must demonstrate measurable impact through programs, initiatives, or policies that support women’s empowerment and gender equality. Countries Eligible to Apply This is a global award, meaning applicants from all countries and regions are welcome to apply. Eligible regions include: Africa Asia Europe North America South America The Middle East Oceania The award aims to recognize impactful initiatives from both developed and developing countries, highlighting diverse solutions to gender equality challenges worldwide. Qualifications and Eligibility Criteria While the award is open to a wide range of applicants, candidates must demonstrate that their initiative or work contributes significantly to women’s empowerment. Key eligibility considerations include: 1. Demonstrated Impact Applicants must show tangible results from their initiatives, such as policy reforms, increased participation of women in leadership, economic empowerment programs, or social reforms. 2. Innovative Approaches The award values innovative and creative solutions that address gender inequality in new and effective ways. 3. Sustainability Projects and initiatives should demonstrate long-term sustainability and the potential for continued impact. 4. Replicability Programs that can be replicated in other countries or communities may receive strong consideration, as they help scale solutions globally. How to Apply Interested applicants can submit their application through the official website by following these steps: Visit the official award website. Navigate to the Apply Here section. Select the appropriate award category. Complete the application form with details about your initiative. Submit supporting documentation demonstrating impact and results. Applications must be completed online via the official platform. Selection Process Applications submitted for the Princess Sabeeka Global Award undergo a rigorous evaluation process conducted by an international jury composed of experts in gender equality and development. The evaluation focuses on: Innovation and creativity Demonstrated impact Sustainability and scalability Contribution to gender equality and women’s empowerment The jury includes internationally recognized experts, policymakers, and leaders working in the field of women’s rights and empowerment. Why This Award Matters Gender equality remains one of the most critical development challenges globally. Despite significant progress, women still face barriers in education, employment, leadership, and access to resources. Awards such as the Princess Sabeeka Global Award play an important role by: Recognizing leaders driving positive change Encouraging governments and organizations to adopt gender-inclusive policies Promoting successful models of women’s empowerment Amplifying global awareness of gender equality initiatives By highlighting successful programs and visionary leaders, the award contributes to the broader international effort to achieve sustainable development and inclusive growth. Deadline Applicants must submit their applications before: 20th March 2026 (11:59 PM New York Time) The Princess Sabeeka Global Award for Women’s Empowerment represents one of the world’s most important recognitions for initiatives that transform the lives of women and girls. By offering substantial financial support and international recognition, the award encourages governments, organizations, and individuals to develop innovative solutions that advance gender equality. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Princess Sabeeka Global Award for Women’s Empowerment 2026: A $100,000 Global Opportunity Recognizing Leaders Advancing Gender Equality appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Empowering Global Women in Management Program 2026 (GWIM 76) in Washington DC
summary: The Empowering Global Women in Management Program 2026 (GWIM 76) is an exceptional leadership development opportunity designed to strengthen the economic leadership and management capacity of women around the world. Implemented by Counterpart International with generous support from the ExxonMobil Foundation , this prestigious program equips women leaders with the knowledge, networks, and tools needed to advance inclusive economic growth and social change. Launched in 2005, the Global Women in Management Program (GWIM) has become a leading initiative dedicated to building the leadership capacity of women working in civil society organizations, social enterprises, and institutions that support women’s economic empowerment. The GWIM 76 cohort for 2026 will bring together 20 women leaders from across the globe for an intensive hybrid leadership program that combines in-person training in Washington, DC with ongoing online mentorship and learning sessions. The program aims to address persistent barriers faced by women in economic leadership by strengthening decision-making capacity, increasing access to professional networks and resources, and enhancing the ability of women leaders to grow sustainable enterprises and organizations. The application deadline is April 30, 2026. Global Leadership Opportunity Through GWIM The Global Women in Management Program is designed as a long-term investment in women’s leadership development. The program integrates leadership training, executive coaching, mentorship, and global networking opportunities to support women leaders who are working to expand economic opportunities for women in their communities. GWIM builds on over 40 years of experience in women’s leadership development, combining practical management training with innovative approaches that help participants adapt to rapidly evolving economic environments. Participants learn how to navigate complex organizational challenges, build sustainable programs, and influence policies that support women’s economic empowerment. Hybrid Learning Structure of the GWIM Program The GWIM 76 program will take place through a hybrid learning model that includes both in-person and online sessions. In-Person Leadership Training The program will begin with an intensive two-week leadership training in Washington, DC from October 19 to October 30, 2026. During this period, participants will engage with expert facilitators, international development professionals, and fellow women leaders from around the world. This immersive training experience includes leadership workshops, collaborative learning sessions, and networking opportunities. Participants will also gain exposure to global perspectives on women’s economic advancement and leadership. Online Learning and Mentorship Following the in-person training, participants will continue their learning through online training sessions, mentorship programs, and collaborative meetings. These sessions will take place from December 2026 to May 2027, totaling approximately 16 to 20 hours of online engagement. Online sessions will focus on leadership development, technology integration, and strategies for organizational growth. Core Learning Areas in the GWIM Program The program curriculum focuses on strengthening leadership capabilities and improving organizational performance. Key learning areas include: Leadership Development Participants will explore different dimensions of leadership, including personal leadership style, emotional intelligence, and confidence-building strategies. The program encourages participants to reflect on their leadership approach while developing strategies for influencing positive change within their organizations. Institutional Sustainability The program provides insights into donor engagement, fundraising strategies, and financial sustainability models that help organizations maintain long-term impact. Participants will also learn how to cultivate and sustain relationships with donors and partners. Economic Advancement Participants will develop strategic planning skills that support business and organizational growth. The program incorporates tools such as the Business Canvas Model to help leaders design scalable and sustainable initiatives. Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technologies The program introduces participants to AI-enabled learning tools and digital platforms that can improve research, data analysis, and innovation. Participants will also explore ethical considerations surrounding artificial intelligence and technology-driven economic transformation. Practicum and Action Plans Each participant will design a personalized action plan to implement leadership and organizational development strategies within their workplace. These action plans allow participants to apply their new skills directly to real-world challenges. Post-Program Mentorship and Global Network One of the most valuable aspects of the GWIM program is the long-term support available to participants after completing the training. Each participant is paired with a GWIM alumnae mentor who provides guidance on implementing leadership action plans and addressing professional challenges. Graduates also join a global network of more than 1,000 GWIM alumnae, creating opportunities for collaboration, knowledge sharing, and partnerships across countries. The program also provides continued access to: Leadership resources Training opportunities Fellowship programs Funding opportunities These networks support ongoing leadership development and professional advancement. Who Should Apply for the GWIM Program The Global Women in Management Program is specifically designed for early- to mid-career women leaders working in organizations that support women’s economic empowerment. The program targets professionals working in: Local NGOs Cooperatives Businesswomen’s associations Microcredit organizations Social enterprises STEAM initiatives supporting women and girls Participants should have experience designing or implementing programs that promote economic opportunities for women. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following requirements: Minimum five years of experience in development programming Minimum three years of experience working on women’s economic empowerment initiatives Strong English language proficiency Ability to participate fully in in-person and online sessions Participants must also be available to travel to Washington, DC for the two-week in-person training. Eligible Countries Women from the following countries are eligible to apply: Brazil Egypt Guyana India Indonesia Kazakhstan Mexico Mozambique China Papua New Guinea Qatar Saudi Arabia Vietnam United Arab Emirates Application Process Applicants must complete the online application form and submit the required documents. As part of the application process, candidates must also provide a Supervisor Endorsement Form confirming support from their organization. The endorsement form may be submitted after the application deadline but must be received by May 5, 2026. Deadline for Applications All applications must be submitted by 11:59 PM EST on April 30, 2026. Late applications will not be considered. Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Empowering Global Women in Management Program 2026 (GWIM 76) opportunity and access the official application page, click here. APPLY HERE Conclusion The Global Women in Management Program 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for women leaders working in economic empowerment and social development to strengthen their leadership capabilities and expand their global networks. Through intensive training, mentorship, and collaboration with women leaders from around the world, participants gain the skills and confidence needed to advance economic opportunities for women and drive meaningful social change. Discover More Fellowship Opportunities For more fellowship opportunities on OFY Opportunities, click here .
