title: Transformative Leadership Director Job 2026 at Teach For All – Global Leadership Accelerator
summary: The Transformative Leadership Director Job 2026 at Teach For All’s Global Leadership Accelerator (GLA) presents a remarkable opportunity for experienced professionals who are passionate about reshaping education systems globally. This remote leadership position allows individuals to work with one of the world’s most influential education networks while supporting thousands of leaders dedicated to creating equitable learning opportunities for children. Teach For All is a global network of more than 60 independent organizations, supporting over 15,000 teachers currently working in classrooms and nearly 125,000 alumni leaders across multiple countries. These leaders collectively impact millions of children worldwide, working to address systemic inequities in education. The Leadership Director for the Global Leadership Accelerator will play a crucial role in designing leadership development experiences, coaching senior leaders, and shaping global initiatives that accelerate system-wide change. Through this position, the selected candidate will work with social entrepreneurs, educators, policymakers, and changemakers across the globe. This job opportunity is remote and available to candidates located in several Asia-Pacific countries, including Bangladesh, Hong Kong, India, Indonesia, Malaysia, Pakistan, the Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand. Leadership Role in the Global Leadership Accelerator The Leadership Director will work within the Global Leadership Accelerator, a strategic initiative that focuses on cultivating strong leadership across the Teach For All network. The Accelerator aims to build deeply conscious leaders who are motivated by purpose and committed to transforming inequitable systems in education. By developing leadership capacity among educators, social entrepreneurs, and policymakers, the program seeks to drive meaningful change in global education systems. This role also connects closely with the Global Institute for Shaping a Better Future, an initiative that facilitates learning and collaboration among education leaders, researchers, and policymakers around the world. The institute contributes to the development of leadership skills while also generating insights, research, and tools that support the transformation of education systems globally. Key Responsibilities of the Leadership Director The Leadership Director will help launch new initiatives and expand existing leadership development programs across the Teach For All network. Some of the most important responsibilities include designing leadership experiences and supporting senior leaders who are working to reshape education systems. Key responsibilities include: Designing Leadership Programs The selected candidate will help co-design leadership development initiatives that bring together emerging and established leaders across the global education sector. Examples include: Designing fellowships for policymakers and political leaders Creating leadership development programs for educators and system leaders Facilitating global learning experiences and workshops Coaching Senior Leaders The Leadership Director will coach and support senior leaders within the Teach For All network. This involves: Leading peer learning groups Supporting leadership teams in strengthening their impact Providing guidance on leadership development and strategic thinking Facilitating Global Learning Experiences Another important responsibility involves facilitating workshops and leadership programs for educators and changemakers worldwide. These learning experiences may include both in-person and virtual formats. Managing Strategic Leadership Initiatives The Leadership Director will manage projects from conception to execution. This includes: Program design Communications and marketing Participant enrollment Program delivery Monitoring impact and outcomes Building Partnerships The role also involves developing partnerships with external organizations and collaborators to co-create flagship leadership programs. These partnerships help expand the reach and effectiveness of leadership development initiatives. Qualifications Required for the Leadership Director Role Teach For All is seeking experienced professionals with strong expertise in leadership development and organizational transformation. The ideal candidate will possess: Extensive experience in leadership development or organizational development Strong coaching and facilitation skills Experience working with senior leaders or executive teams Expertise in designing learning experiences for adults Exceptional strategic thinking and problem-solving abilities Candidates should also demonstrate the ability to work effectively in global environments and collaborate with diverse teams across cultures and regions. Additional Experience and Skills While not mandatory, the following experience is considered highly valuable: Background in education leadership or school leadership Experience in community organizing or systems change Expertise in diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives Experience designing programs at scale Familiarity with the Teach For All network or one of its partner organizations Teach For All is particularly interested in candidates who demonstrate an entrepreneurial mindset and the ability to envision and launch innovative leadership programs. Work Environment and Travel Expectations This role is fully remote, allowing professionals to work from one of several eligible countries across the Asia-Pacific region. Although the position is primarily virtual, the role may require up to 20% travel to support leadership programs, workshops, or global learning initiatives. Because the organization operates globally, team members may occasionally need to work across different time zones. Benefits of the Leadership Director Role Teach For All offers a supportive work environment and a strong commitment to staff well-being and development. Benefits include: Opportunities for professional growth and leadership development Flexible work arrangements and remote work Generous leave policies Comprehensive employee benefits Opportunities to contribute to global education transformation Employees also become part of a collaborative global network of education leaders dedicated to ensuring every child has access to quality education. Application Process Interested candidates should submit the following documents: Resume or Curriculum Vitae (CV) A one-page motivation letter written in English The motivation letter should explain the applicant’s interest in the role and how their experience aligns with the responsibilities of the position. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early applications are strongly encouraged. Work Authorization Requirement Teach For All does not provide visa sponsorship or immigration assistance for this role. Applicants must already have legal authorization to work in the country where they are applying. Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Transformative Leadership Director Job 2026 at Teach For All’s Global Leadership Accelerator, visit the official application page here . APPLY HERE Conclusion The Leadership Director position within Teach For All’s Global Leadership Accelerator represents an exceptional opportunity for experienced professionals who are passionate about education transformation and leadership development. Through this role, the selected candidate will contribute to shaping leadership across one of the largest global networks working to ensure that all children have access to quality education.
